name: Pot Roast of Your Choice
recipeIngredient: 4 pounds boneless beef chuck roast, trimmed
recipeInstructions:
1. Start with a basic pot roast recipe.
2. Choose a country and create a flavor profile for a delicious dinner entrée with a foreign accent.
3. Preheat the oven to 325°F (165°C).
4. In a 5-quart Dutch oven, heat the oil over medium heat. Pat the meat dry with paper towels.
5. Add the meat to the hot oil along with flavored vegetables.
6. Brown the meat on all sides, stirring vegetables occasionally.
7. Add the liquids and seasonings. Cover and bake for 2-2/1 hours to 3 hours, or until the meat is fork-tender.
8. Transfer the meat to a cutting board. Cover loosely with foil and let stand for 10 to 15 minutes.
9. Discard any bay leaves and citrus peel and skim the fat from the sauce.
10. If desired, thicken the sauce by stirring in 3 tablespoons flour mixed with 1/4 cup water.
11. Slice the meat across the grain and serve with the sauce.
French:
Vegetables: 1 1/2 cups thinly sliced leeks
3/4 cup each chopped carrots and celery
1 tsp minced garlic
Seasonings: 1 tsp salt
1/2 tsp each pepper and dried thyme leaves
2 small bay leaves
Liquids: 1 cup dry red wine
2 tbsp red wine vinegar
Eastern Mediterranean:
Vegetables: 1 cup chopped onion
1 tbsp minced garlic
Seasonings: 1 tsp ground cumin
1 tsp salt
1/2 tsp pepper
1/2 tsp cinnamon
2 bay leaves
Liquids: 1 cup dry red wine or beef broth mixed with 2 tbsp tomato paste
